summary: Created MPI-160 — surface per-GPU system-RAM tiers in the RunPod picker (lowestPrice.minMemory hides higher-RAM hosts; LTX warm-perf is RAM-gated ~90GB floor). Research spike first: is per-host RAM exposable via RunPod API?

Reframed: RunPod advertises only a RAM FLOOR per GPU/DC (their console shows 60GB for the 5090 in EU-RO-1); actual host RAM varies and exceeded it (~90GB) on a prior connect in the SAME DC. So picker cannot surface a higher tier (none listed). Real lever = detect actual Pod RAM post-connect via /wrapper/stats + warn when under the ~90GB LTX floor (optional reconnect-to-reroll). Title/desc updated.

Refined (user): SAME DC (EU-RO-1) has BOTH 60GB and ~90GB 5090 hosts, hit several times each. So multiple RAM configs DO exist per GPU/DC; lowestPrice just surfaces the cheapest currently-available, hiding the pricier high-RAM host. Card now has a 3-rung feasibility ladder: A post-connect detect+warn (always works, ship first), B wait-for-high-RAM reusing the existing auto-retry toggle (if API exposes a minMemory constraint/offerings list), C picker hint. Spike the RunPod API offerings/minMemory query before B/C.

Pricing refinement (user): RunPod 5090 price is FIXED per card, same across RAM configs AND data centers. So lowestPrice is NOT hiding a pricier high-RAM host (price-tied). Most likely: lowestPrice.minMemory is a LIVE availability signal (reads 60 or 90 depending on what host is free now). This makes lever B (wait-for-high-RAM, reuse auto-retry toggle) the clear winner — flat price = zero cost to wait for the 90GB host, just poll minMemory until >=90 then deploy. Spike: confirm minMemory actually varies over time + whether deploy accepts a min-RAM constraint.

PIVOT (user): goal is now LIST EVERY available RAM config per GPU as its own picker row (RTX 5090 · 60GB + RTX 5090 · 90GB = two rows, RAM + VRAM shown per row), direct selection — NOT the old wait-for/detect levers. Investigated (read-only): both RunPod GraphQL queries the app uses COLLAPSE hosts into one number (lowestPrice.minMemory single scalar; gpuAvailability = available bool + stockStatus only), no per-host config list, and deploy sends no min-RAM constraint. BUT RunPod introspection is DISABLED (MPI-77), so undocumented fields may exist. Card rewritten to 2 stages: Stage 1 LIVE API PROBE (gate) — test host-listing/offerings query + probe podFindAndDeployOnDemand for minMemoryInGb against a real key in EU-RO-1; Stage 2 build branches on probe (fan real rows / synthesize RAM-tier rows w/ deploy constraint / impossible→fall back to old warn+poll levers). Title+desc+tags updated.

STAGE 1 API PROBE DONE (live, read-only, no pod; research/api-probe-2026-07-01.md). (1) NO host-listing query — lowestPrice is one collapsed scalar (5090=60GB), host-count fields return null, gpuAvailability is one row/GPU/DC, availableGpuTypes doesn't exist. => 'list every RAM config as a row' IMPOSSIBLE as stated. (2) minMemoryInGb IS an accepted podFindAndDeployOnDemand input (schema-valid), but placement effect UNPROVEN — all failures collapse to generic SUPPLY_CONSTRAINT, only a REAL deploy proves it. REFRAMED: if a live minMemoryInGb:90 deploy lands >=90GB → add RAM-TIER picker rows + pass minMemoryInGb into create spec; else fall back to lever A warn + lever B poll. ONE remaining unknown = the live deploy proof (burns a brief pod, user-gated).

LIVE DEPLOY PROOF DONE — FEASIBLE. 4 real ephemeral 5090s in EU-RO-1 (created+RAM-read+deleted each; orphan sweep reaped:[]): minMemoryInGb is HONORED as a hard placement FLOOR. baseline/16/90 all created (landed 92GB); minMemoryInGb:200 FAILED SUPPLY_CONSTRAINT — only RAM value varied → RunPod filters by it. FINAL SHAPE: not a live 60-vs-90 list (API can't enumerate), but ONE high-RAM variant row 'RTX 5090 · ≥90GB (LTX)' passing minMemoryInGb:90 → guaranteed ≥90GB or clean SUPPLY_CONSTRAINT (pairs w/ existing wait-for-stock; flat price=free to wait). A ~60GB row isn't worth building (floor still accepts 90GB host). Build: add minMemoryInGb to create spec BOTH paths + picker variant; pre-ship confirm REST POST /pods accepts it (only GraphQL proven live). research ADDENDUM has the table + exact lines.

BUILT (design pivot per user): dropped LTX-specific rows → added a 'Min system RAM (GB)' input under the GPU picker (future-proof for any model's RAM need). storage.js minRamGb + normalize; MpiSettings input+connect-body+ramFloorMissed honest message (auto-retry ON=wait, OFF=stop); remoteProxy _createPodInternal sets spec.minMemoryInGb + routes high-RAM creates via GraphQL (proven path, REST unproven) + create/reconnect read+pass+surface ramFloorMissed; runpodRemote createPodGraphql maps it. node+ESLint clean, app boots clean. validation.md has the test script. NOT user-verified.

doing→done, complete/accepted. USER-VERIFIED live: floor 90 lands 92GB pod; floor 200 → no pod + honest message 'No host with ≥200 GB system RAM for NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 in EU-RO-1 right now…' + HEADS UP toast, no bill. UI polished (inline Min System RAM [90] GB, future-proof hint). Committed 2b3f182 (RunPod branch).
Archived completed card during board cleanup.
